solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int distinctSequences(int n) {
        const int MOD = 1e9 + 7;
        if (n == 1) return 6;

        // dp[i][prev][prev_prev] = number of valid sequences of length i ending with prev and prev_prev
        // Since n can be up to 10^4, we optimize space by using only two layers: current and previous
        int dp[2][7][7] = {0}; // 0-6 for values, 0 for no previous

        // Initialize for n = 2
        for (int i = 1; i <= 6; ++i) {
            for (int j = 1; j <= 6; ++j) {
                if (__gcd(i, j) == 1) {
                    dp[0][i][j] = 1;
                }
            }
        }

        int curr = 0, next = 1;
        for (int i = 3; i <= n; ++i) {
            for (int prev = 1; prev <= 6; ++prev) {
                for (int prev_prev = 1; prev_prev <= 6; ++prev_prev) {
                    if (prev == prev_prev) continue; // Ensure at least a gap of 2 between equal values
                    for (int new_val = 1; new_val <= 6; ++new_val) {
                        if (new_val == prev) continue; // Ensure at least a gap of 2 between equal values
                        if (__gcd(new_val, prev) == 1) {
                            dp[next][prev][new_val] = (dp[next][prev][new_val] + dp[curr][prev_prev][prev]) % MOD;
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
            std::swap(curr, next);
            for (int i = 0; i <= 6; ++i) {
                for (int j = 0; j <= 6; ++j) {
                    dp[next][i][j] = 0;
                }
            }
        }

        int result = 0;
        for (int i = 1; i <= 6; ++i) {
            for (int j = 1; j <= 6; ++j) {
                result = (result + dp[curr][i][j]) % MOD;
            }
        }

        return (n == 1) ? 6 : result;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n * 6^3)
space complexity: O(6^2)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses dynamic programming with optimized space to count valid sequences. It tracks sequences ending with two previous values to ensure the gap constraint. The GCD condition is checked for adjacent values. The time complexity is O(n * 6^3) due to nested loops over dice values, and space is O(6^2) due to the DP table.>
